headline: Anti-hopping law has 3 loopholes parties already exploit
context: The 2022 anti-party-hopping constitutional amendment contains three identified loopholes that allow MPs to effectively switch allegiances without triggering vacancy provisions.

cards.big: Anti-hopping law has 3 loopholes — 7 MPs already used them since 2023
cards.sub: Constitutional amendment meant to end party-hopping is being circumvented

cards.big: Article 49A exempts MPs expelled by their own party from vacancy trigger
cards.sub: Parties can 'expel' willing members to circumvent anti-hopping provisions legally

cards.big: 2020 Sheraton Move involved 39 MPs — new law wouldn't have prevented 23 of those switches
cards.sub: Current law addresses only voluntary resignation, not orchestrated party splits

cards.big: Election Commission has no investigative power to determine if defection was engineered
cards.sub: Enforcement depends on party self-reporting — the foxes guard the henhouse

cards.big: An anti-hopping law with loopholes big enough for 7 MPs is a speed bump, not a barrier
Should the anti-hopping law be amended to close the party-expulsion loophole?
